pattern: Processing the fixed cycle of multiply, floor-divide, add, subtract while respecting operator precedence reduces to evaluating each multiply-divide chunk then combining chunks with signs.
hints: The operations repeat in a fixed cycle, and the multiply and divide steps bind tighter than the add and subtract.
Group each consecutive run that starts with a multiply and a floor-divide into one value first.

Evaluate the first group as it stands, then every later group is subtracted while the lone plus terms between groups are added back, mind that floor division here truncates toward negative infinity only on the grouped positive products.

edge_inputs.description: small case ending with a plus
boundary where the trailing operations matter
another remainder class of the cycle

stress.description: random sizes across the input range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 10000)))]
stress.note: single integer argument; reference handles the full range
